displayNameVi: Bệnh đốm tảo trên lá sầu riêng
shortDescription: Algal leaf spots can appear as raised orange, rusty, or gray-green patches on durian leaves, especially in humid conditions.
careNote: Improve airflow around the canopy, remove heavily affected leaves, avoid overhead watering, and ask a local agricultural expert before applying any treatment.

shortDescription: Leaf blight usually causes brown or scorched-looking areas that may expand from leaf edges or spots.
careNote: Keep leaves dry when possible, prune damaged foliage, reduce plant stress, and verify the diagnosis with a specialist before choosing treatment.

displayNameVi: Bệnh Colletotrichum trên lá sầu riêng
shortDescription: Colletotrichum infections often produce dark necrotic spots or anthracnose-like lesions on leaves.
careNote: Remove infected plant material, keep the growing area clean, improve drainage and airflow, and use this result only as an initial screening signal.

shortDescription: The model classifies the uploaded leaf as visually healthy within the current six-class durian model.
careNote: Continue routine monitoring, keep the tree well cared for, and retest if symptoms appear or the image quality is unclear.

displayNameVi: Bệnh Phomopsis trên lá sầu riêng
shortDescription: Phomopsis disease can cause leaf spotting, tissue dieback, or lesions that may worsen under humid conditions.
careNote: Prune affected material, avoid prolonged leaf wetness, maintain sanitation, and confirm with local agronomy guidance before treatment.

displayNameVi: Bệnh Rhizoctonia trên lá sầu riêng
shortDescription: Rhizoctonia-related symptoms may include irregular lesions or blighted tissue, often favored by wet and crowded conditions.
careNote: Improve spacing and airflow, avoid excess moisture, remove affected debris, and treat the result as decision support rather than a final diagnosis.
